description_long: This is a tall, narrow filing cabinet with a light wood grain finish, designed with a pentagonal footprint that allows it to fit neatly into a corner. The cabinet features a single, full-height door with a subtle vertical seam, suggesting a concealed handle or push-to-open mechanism. The top surface is flat and slightly overhangs the front, providing a small ledge. The wood texture is consistent across all visible surfaces, giving it a modern and minimalist appearance. There are no visible external handles, label holders, or locks, contributing to its clean and streamlined look. This cabinet is likely intended for storing files or documents in a compact, space-efficient manner.
description: A tall, light wood grain filing cabinet with a pentagonal shape and a single, seamless door, designed for corner placement. Its minimalist design features no visible handles or label holders.
description_view_0: The cabinet is seen from an angle, highlighting the overhanging top ledge and the vertical seam of the door on the right side.
description_view_1: This view shows the cabinet from the front, displaying the flat top and the uninterrupted wood grain of the main door panel.
description_view_2: The cabinet is viewed from the opposite side, revealing the door seam on the left and the continuous wood texture.
description_view_3: A direct view of the front face, emphasizing the pentagonal shape and the seamless, handle-free door.
